Investigative Media Storytelling · untimely Records 2026
2X

纪实调查

When was the last time you watched a whole show at normal speed?

We looked into something that a lot of people didn't notice: Pacing the video at a faster speed is not a habit you came up with on your own.It was the platform that gradually trained you into this state.

Investigation Platform:：Tencent TV · TikTok

" I don't want to speed up, but the normal speed makes me anxious." 
- Interviewee Cassie

01. Everyone is speeding up, but no one remembers when it all began...

Open Tencent Video, iQiyi, or just scroll through TikTok, and you'll notice something quite normal: everyone's watching at double speed. 1.5x, 2x, and some folks are even going for 3x. The platforms call this "user preference" and say it shows that modern people are better at managing their time. But! we would like to ask a different question: Do you really want to move faster, or has slowing down already become unbearable for you?

We discovered that there is a very long chain behind this. From deciding how to make money on the platform, to the shape of the series, and then to your feelings, each step is closely linked to the next. Now, let's start from the beginning...

one

What the platform cares most about is not whether you like this drama or not, but how long you stay on the app. The longer you stay, the more advertising revenue you generate.

two

We have noticed that in recent TV series, the actual time spent telling the story has been getting shorter, while the amount of time spent delaying the plot and reiterating emotions has been increasing, especially in the final few episodes.

发现三

TikTok's large-scale data shows that users are spending more and more time on the platform, but the proportion of people who actually watch through a video has not increased over the past eight years – it has remained at around 45% and has never changed.

four

The fast-forward function of Tencent Video has a default "long press speed" of 2 times. The AI intelligent fast-forward feature will automatically determine which scenes are "not worth" being watched normally. You think you are making a choice, but the direction has already been set.

02 Issues with the series

The TV series gets longer and longer, but there are fewer and fewer stories.

" I rarely watch long videos now because the plots are too drawn-out. "

— Interviewee Evelyn

Do you have this feeling: watching a drama, even though you're following it, you feel like nothing happened? 
This is not your illusion. We actually measured TV series from different eras, recording how much time was spent pushing the story forward and how much time was spent "filling in time" in each episode.

The result is clear: in recent years, there has been an increasing amount of time filling in the drama. For example, in 2016, "Dark Side" spent an average of about 75% of each episode telling stories. 
But can you believe it? By 2024, in "Days of a Police Officer", this number has dropped to 46% - filling content has skyrocketed by 33%, and almost all of it is concentrated in the last few episodes.

来源：腾讯视频内容分析 · 暗黑者(2016) vs 警察荣誉(2024)
深色是真正在讲故事的时间，浅色是填充。可以看到：左边的2016年剧几乎全是深色，右边的2024年剧——尤其结尾——几乎翻转了。

The comparison between "Joy of Life" and "Dafeng Night Watch" is more direct: both are blockbusters launched by the same publisher, with the same luxurious cast and the same popular theme. However, five years have passed, and the actual storytelling time per episode has decreased by nearly four minutes on average.

来源：腾讯视频内容分析 · 庆余年(2019) vs 大奉打更人(2024)
左三列是《庆余年》，始终保持高密度。右三列是《大奉打更人》，到结局集时填充已经接近一半。

We're not saying which drama is particularly bad. The trend of several TV dramas we compared is the same: the more recent the drama, the thinner the story at the end.

来源：腾讯视频结构分析 · 2016年 vs 2024年
蓝线（2016年）：中段达到峰值，结尾依然稳定。橙线（2024年）：中段已经大幅下滑，结尾直接跌穿——70%都是填充内容。

TV dramas are not declining, and their output is even higher. But there are fewer TV dramas with essence. The extra TV dramas are not for you - they are reserved for the platform's advertising space.

03 Platform · Speed Doubling Function and Algorithm Design

The platform gave you the remote control, but sorry - it has already adjusted the direction for you.

" I thought I was controlling my viewing speed. BUT who the heck set the default playback to 2x speed? "

— Interviewee Sheryl

As the plot of the drama becomes increasingly sluggish, the audience will naturally want to speed it up. But the platform did not solve the problem of "drama being too slow" - So they did something smarter: It directly added the 2x speed function and quietly set default values for you.

Long-press the screen to default to 2x speed. The "AI intelligent double speed" function will automatically judge which stories are the leading role and which are the supporting role, and then skip them for you. You haven't entered any instructions - but you are already watching the play in the way that the platform thinks you "should" use.

截图证据 基础倍速界面
长按速度默认 3.0X，"长按上滑锁定倍速"默认开启——你随手一按，就已经在三倍速了。
截图证据 AI智能倍速界面
系统自动识别演员（黄亦玫），高能剧情 1.0X 保留，其他片段 1.5X 加速。你"选择"的，只是在平台已判断好的基础上微调。
SVIP 专属

Do you want to control the speed of a specific actor or segment separately? That requires opening a membership.

This is not just a functional design - it demonstrates that the platform clearly knows that speed is what users truly need. It packages' needs' as rights, making you pay for 'fast forward'. Behind the double speed is a pair of platform hands, pushing you to use it and benefiting from it.

On TikTok, we have discovered more interesting situations ⬇️:

来源：TikTok用户行为研究 · 347名用户 · 920万条记录
蓝线（观看时长）和橙线（点赞量）在120天内翻了一倍多。但绿线——把视频真正看完的比例——一直平着，从来没动过，停在45%附近。

So the platform doesn't care if you've finished watching it or not. What it cares about is whether you continue to stay. As long as you're still browsing, the follow-up ads are still waiting for you, and the money is still making money - They don't care when you've watched the whole video or not.

When the backend data told the platform that "no one really finished watching the video, but everyone continued to watch it", the platform's conclusion was "we won't waste most of our time improving the content, but focus on keeping users' fingers scrolling on the screen." The platform is guiding us to just keep scrolling on the screen, and the next video will be what you want to watch.

来源：TikTok平台机制分析 从"让你看完"到"让你待着"
完播率低但用户还在刷 → 放弃优化完播率 → 转向优化停留时长。平台的目标，已经从"让你满意"变成了"让你走不了"。

The platform is actually quietly telling you that most of the content is not worth reading seriously.

04 thinking · How everything comes together

No one gave the command, but that's just how things unfolded, step by step.

" I just... can't get back to normal speed."

— Interviewee Tristan

The interesting thing is that no one has ever said, 'I want the audience to go 2x'. The platform just wants to make more money, the producers just want the show to be broadcast, and the users just want to watch a show. Everyone is doing what is most reasonable for themselves. But these 'reasonable things' combined become a cycle that nobody intentionally designed, but is steadily operating.

第一步

平台定规则

平台决定：谁让用户待得越久，谁就能拿到更多推荐、更多广告收入。内容好不好不重要，待多久才重要。

第二步

剧集跟着变

拍剧的人知道了这个规则，就开始往剧情里加更多填充——重复的对话、慢镜头回忆、没必要的情绪铺垫。剧越长，钱越多。

第三步

观众开始烦

观众发现剧越来越拖，开始觉得烦、觉得没意思，然后开始倍速、快进、跳集。我们问卷里，55%的人说是因为"内容太注水"。

第四步

平台说：没问题

你开始倍速看，平台的数据显示你还在看、还在"停留"。倍速不影响广告收入，停留时长照样计算。平台完全不亏。

第五步

感觉变了

用了一段时间倍速之后，正常速度开始让你感到不舒服。你的感觉被重新校准了：快是正常，慢是浪费时间。

第六步

回到第一步

平台看到"快节奏内容更受欢迎"，就推更多快节奏内容。拍剧的人也跟着调整。循环继续，一圈一圈转下去。

来源：TikTok平台机制研究 · 加速循环示意图
内容节奏变快 → 用户习惯快节奏 → 采用倍速 → 对慢节奏失去耐心 → 推动平台继续加速。每一圈转完，你都离"能慢下来"更远一点。

Everyone is driving at double speed, not because it's more enjoyable and time-saving— it's because the dragging plot and filler contents make the user unable to hold on anymore.

We Found
05 Conclusion

The remote control is in your hand,
But what you can choose,
There aren't many left.

" No one is forcing you to double your speed. You can always adjust your speed back to single, and no one is stopping you. "

— That statement sounds right, but something doesn't sit right.

But when you really try to slow down, you will find that the drama is dragging and the content is repeating. Your patience is telling you that slowing down has become something that requires deliberate persistence, rather than being taken for granted.

We have a question in our questionnaire: After reading a paragraph at double speed, can you usually recall specific emotional details? 

40.45% of people believe that they rarely recall details. This indicates that playing at double speed will reduce people's emotional perception ability.

来源：问卷调查 Q10 · 178名受访者
40.45%的人说很少能回忆起具体的情绪细节，28.65%说偶尔能，只有17.98%说能。他们看了，但那些需要时间生长的情绪，被跳过去了。

Another question: Do you think you started using double speed because the content was too slow, or because you were trained by the platform to do so?
55% of people's first reaction is' the content is too long ', and 51% say it's' time-saving'. But these two reasons actually mean the same thing - the content made them unable to stay, not that they had already wanted to be quick.

来源：问卷调查 Q4 · 178名受访者
55%的人第一反应是"内容太拖"，51%说是"省时间"。但这两个理由，其实说的是同一件事——内容让他们待不下去了，而不是他们本来就想快。

" Algorithms are not just responding to your preferences - they are creating your preferences. You think you are choosing, but you are actually adapting. "

— Result

What we are investigating is something without bad people. Platforms, creators, and users everyone is making reasonable choices for themselves. But the result is that the way we all watch videos has quietly changed in the past few years.

We interviewed six users from different backgrounds. They use their own experiences to tell everyone that double speed is not a problem, it is a symptom. Something is going wrong behind the scenes - it's not because the content is bad, it's not because they don't have time, it's because they're used to it. They don't remember how long it took for the platform to cultivate this habit.

调查核心数据

2016→2024年，长剧内容密度最大降幅−38.7%↓ 将近四成
2024年部分剧集结尾"填充内容"占比≈70%大部分都在拖
TikTok用户观看时长增长（120天内）×2.2但完播率从没提高
开倍速的用户说"为了节省时间"79.26%最多被提及
倍速后很少能回忆情绪细节的受访者40.45%看了，但没被触动

@Vicky

" Watching the entire drama at double speed, I actually don't remember a few details. But if someone asks me, 'Have you watched this drama?' I would say yes. Is it counted? "


This investigation is based on 178 questionnaire data, Tencent Video content density analysis, and TikTok platform mechanism research

untimely Records · Investigative Media Storytelling · EST. 2026
